# STRATA Swap-In Report — aviation competency suite over the INDUCED ontology

*Generated by `scripts/swapin_eval.py` on 2026-06-12 (full fixtures).*

The swap-in (whitepaper §11.3 Phase 3): `ontoforge materialize --ontology induced`
materializes the estate via the GENERIC engine from the ontology STRATA induced —
no gold ontology, no hand-built mapping — and `ask` answers over that world.

## Gates

| gate | requirement | result |
|---|---|---|
| answerable correct (induced) | >= 8/15 | **11/15** PASS |
| answerable correct (gold)    | reference | 15/15 |
| citation coverage on answered cells | 100% | 100% PASS |
| confidently wrong (conf >= tau_high 0.92) | 0 | 0 PASS |
| unanswerables abstain | 2/2 | 2/2 PASS |
| trick unit (CQ-18) rejected by type checker | yes | PASS |

## Induced world

- classes induced: 16 (AircraftReference, City, CodeTypeAircraft, Component, DateCityEvent, EngineManufacturerModel, Event, Maintenance, Master, NarrativeModelEvent, OperatorDateEvent, PlaceLocaleReference, Registrant, Report, StateReference, Zip)
- entities 4208, cells 65131, links 6100
- ER[Master]: exact-variant, 0 clusters / 2492 identities over faa_master, maintenance_erp, ntsb_events

## Regression analysis (induced vs gold)

### CQ-03 — semantics gap (temporal identity) + latent-class name capture

*Who was the registrant of tail number N44304 as of 1987-05-20?*

- observed: abstain (rejected by the OQIR type checker: unknown prope...)
- why: Registry key reuse: tail N44304 names two airframes over time, and 'as of 1987-05-20' must pick the one registered THEN. The gold world-builder hand-encodes the semantic that CERT ISSUE DATE -> EXPIRATION DATE is the registration's valid-time window (worldbuild.window()), so as-of execution filters correctly. The generic engine has no licensed evidence for that reading and commits every cell with Interval(0) validity. Compounding it, 'registrant' grounds most strongly to the induced latent Registrant class (the faa_master REGISTRANT NAME 3NF decomp), and the planner's Registrant-target candidates die in the type checker ('unknown property n_number on class Registrant') — every interpretation is rejected and the engine abstains.
- STRATA improvement implied: Induce valid-time anchors: when a table shows identifier reuse (same non-key identifier, multiple rows) with a paired start/end date column whose ranges are disjoint per identifier, emit a temporal-window annotation the materializer turns into real valid Intervals and LODESTONE plans as-of filters against.

### CQ-04 — semantics gap (temporal identity)

*Which airframe serial number did tail number N44304 refer to as of 2024-06-01?*

- observed: WRONG (got ['17238197', '28-1997319'], conf 0.21)
- why: Same key-reuse shape as CQ-03. Here the Master-target reading survives (grounding 0.83) but with Interval(0) validity BOTH registry rows for N44304 project their serials — the correct 17238197 plus the superseded 28-1997319 — at confidence 0.21, far below tau_high: a low-confidence over-answer, not a confidently-wrong one.
- STRATA improvement implied: Same temporal-window induction as CQ-03.

### CQ-11 — missing class (latent operator concept has no extent)

*How many maintenance work orders belong to DELTA AIR LINES INC when all of its ERP operator-name spellings are folded together?*

- observed: clarify
- why: The operator property cluster spans three tables (asrs AIRCRAFT 1 OPERATOR, erp OPERATOR_NAME, ntsb OPERATOR), but STRATA admits it only on a lattice-intersection class (OperatorDateEvent) with NO backing candidate — no plan materializes operator entities, and because the columns are non-unique in their tables no alternate-identity domain forms, so generic ER never folds the ERP spelling variants. 'DELTA AIR LINES INC' then matches two different value surfaces (an unfolded operator spelling vs registrant_name) and the engine asks its one clarification instead of counting over a folded identity. Safe behavior, wrong outcome class.
- STRATA improvement implied: Admit shared multi-table name-cluster domains as latent entity candidates (extent = distinct values of the cluster), so the generic ER cascade folds their spelling variants and counts group over the folded entity — the schema evidence (3-table property cluster) already exists; only candidate emission is missing.

### CQ-15 — grounding miss (abbreviation opacity / property drift)

*How many total fatalities are RECORDED across NTSB events in AK? (Blank INJ_TOT_F cells are unknown, not zero — exclude them.)*

- observed: WRONG (got [5], conf 0.87)
- why: ntsb INJ_TOT_F induces as 'injury_total_f': STRATA's normalizer expands INJ->injury and TOT->total, but the trailing 'F' (fatal) is semantically opaque without domain knowledge, so 'fatalities' cannot ground onto the measure. The planner falls back to the well-typed count-rows reading of 'How many ... across NTSB events in AK' = 5 at confidence 0.87 — under tau_high 0.92, so the calibrated gate holds, but this is the swap-in's most uncomfortable miss: a wrong number delivered with citations.
- STRATA improvement implied: LLM-tier property naming at induction: escalate opaque column abbreviations through the spine (tier-2) for name/synonym proposals grounded in the column's value distribution, recorded as ordinary PropertyDef synonyms (the same surface the identity-synonym enrichment already uses).

## Honest summary

The induced world answers 11/15 vs the gold world's 15/15. Misses: 1 abstain, 1 clarify, 2 wrong.
Wrong answers stay BELOW tau_high 0.92 (max wrong confidence 0.87),
so the calibrated-abstention safety gate holds, but they are real planner misses
— low-confidence fallback readings, not silent hallucinations (see per-question
analysis above). 100% atom-level citation coverage carries over unchanged.
The remaining gap is concentrated in vocabulary opacity (abbreviated source columns
the induced names cannot expand), latent concepts STRATA admits only as un-extended
lattice intersections (the operator domain), and temporal identity (key reuse over
time) — instance-level evidence the pipeline could lift into STRATA annotations.
